analysis.reasoning: Core message: Informational tweet listing the agenda for an upcoming European Council meeting (COVID-19, climate change, relations with Turkey and the U.S., security/terrorism), with a link to meeting info. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story): Not promoted; no historical reflection or success framing. EU2 (EU is united): Implicit coordination could suggest unity, but the tweet does not explicitly emphasize agreement, solidarity, or unity—just an agenda—so not supported. EU3 (useful for citizens): Partially aligned because it highlights the EU addressing key citizen concerns (health, climate, security) and a proactive agenda; even without outcomes, it frames the EU as tackling practical issues. EU4 (avant-garde actor): No explicit leadership or pioneering claims; just topics listed. EU5 (global champion of fair causes): Not addressed. EU6 (contributes to a better world): Mention of external relations could hint at global role, but there’s no claim about contributions or improving the world; insufficient. Conclusion: Only EU3 is supported.
